notes: 25:1-7 Repentant Lamanites Martyred By the Seed of Amulon

quotes.text: The land of Ammonihah] The city of Ammonihah and "every living soul of the Ammonihahites" were destroyed because they martyred the Saints and persecuted the prophets (see Alma 16:9).
quotes.source: Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 3:, p.173

notes: martyrdom Our English word comes from the Greek (martus) "those who prove the genuineness of their faith in Christ by undergoing a violent death".
25:8-12 Abinadi's Prophecy of God's Vengeance Fulfilled
verified, for they were driven by the Lamanites, and they were hunted, and they were smitten.
25:13-17 Seeing the Power of God Upon the Nephites Leads Many Lamanites to Repent

type Sacred scripture both describes actual events and foreshadows future realities. The Savior clearly taught this dual nature of scripture to the Nephites when he commanded them to search the writings of Isaiah, because this ancient prophet "spake as touching all things concerning my people which are of the house of Israel . . . And all things that he spake have been and shall be , even according to the words which he spake " (3 Nephi 23:1-3; emphasis added). This manner of prophesying is called typology. Typology is a divine means to prepare the minds of God's people for the future. It testifies that God's hand is in history and he directs the affairs of his children. Sacred history, therefore, contains specific events that foreshadow the future.
In the latter days the Lord has emphasized again how, in the words of the Book of Mormon, our

quotes.text: Such ultimate hope constitutes the “anchor of the soul” and is retained through the gift of the Holy Ghost and faith in Christ (Hebrews 6:19; see also Alma 25:16; Ether 12:9). In contrast, viewing life without the prospect of immortality can diminish not only hope but also the sense of personal accountability (see 1 Corinthians 15:19; Alma 30:18).
quotes.speaker: Neal A. Maxwell
quotes.attr: Neal A. Maxwell, in Conference Report, Oct. 1998, 78; or Ensign, Nov. 1998, 61

quotes.text: So firm a foundation having been laid for the faith of the Nephite people, we find that in every period of their history they retained their reverence for the law of Moses, though disputations sometimes arose, by reason of iniquity, with regard to its symbolism or its saving quality. The apostates, who separated themselves from the Church, occasionally fell into the grievous error of exalting the law above the Gospel, and, whilst maintaining its divine origin, they ignored its typical value and denied that it was a preparatory system leading to a higher, holier and more perfect law; they refused to recognize it as a schoolmaster to bring them to Christ.
quotes.speaker: John Taylor
quotes.attr: John Taylor, Mediation and Atonement, chapter 14

quotes.text: Whenever the Lord revealed Himself to men in ancient days, and commanded them to offer sacrifice to Him, that it was done that they might look forward in faith to the time of His coming, and rely upon the power of that atonement for a remission of their sins
quotes.speaker: Teachings of the Prophet Joseph Smith
quotes.source: Teachings of the Prophet Joseph Smith
quotes.attr: Teachings of the Prophet Joseph Smith, 60-61
